author: garyrong
content:
Question A: <@1126229231572094976> <@429719902363058177> 

> if the recipient is an EIP-7702 delegated account, additionally charge the delegation-target access in regular gas — COLD_ACCOUNT_ACCESS if cold, WARM_ACCESS if warm.
Shall we also include the delegation in the BAL in this case (runtime gas charging before enterring the first call frame)?

EIP-7928:
> The delegation target MUST NOT be included during delegation creation or modification and MUST only be included once it is actually loaded as an execution target (e.g., via CALL/CALLCODE/DELEGATECALL/STATICCALL under authority execution).
I am not sure for the top-most transaction, if it satisfies this condition as the execution target

author: garyrong
content:
Question B: 
I think we kind of agree to revert the authorization changes if tx fails with runtime gas charging (such as call recipient gas charging, authorization runtime gas charging).

What if the top-most call frame is halted? Shall we also revert authorization changes? Just need the clarification

author: garyrong
content:
Question C: the ordering of pre-existing pre-warming runtime gas charge

We have a few states to be pre-warmed, such as coinbase, tx.sender, tx.to. Just want to confirm we will pre-warm these accounts (and EIP 2930 accessList) before the runtime gas charging, right? The order matters as we need the warmth for charging the delegation access in runtime.

If so, EIP 2780 `Then the recipient account is loaded and added to accessed_addresses` this sentence is irrelevant and can be deleted?

author: draganrakita
content:
My takes on this:
Question A:
We have few examples of this, is there is no gas, dont include it, if gas is spend include it.

Question B:
If top most frame halts, dont touch auth list.

Question C:
I think this is correct and we should assume those accounts are warmed.

author: garyrong
content:
For A, yeah it's a good point to pair the bal inclusion and accessList update (gas charging). So in this case, we **need to include the delegation in the BAL explicitly**.

For B, can you elaborate it? Do you mean the auth list changes **shouldn't be reverted** if topmost frame halts?

For C, I agree we should assume these accounts are pre-warmed. Maybe we need to clarify the 2780 EIP a bit, the tx.to is already warmed when we charge the runtime gas

author: gurukamath
content:
I had a very similar question to B. See [here](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11844#discussion_r3534845959)
Out of the scenarios I outline there, I think only OOG at 1 and 2 should revert the state changes made by the authorizations processing. 3 should not.

author: garyrong
content: I have the same stance as yours, i also think the authorization changes should be kept if top most frame halts, 3 in your example. We need to explicitly mention that in the EIP, and also need the insights from other people

author: misilva73
content: Agree as well. Happy to add this clarification in my clean up PR
